reasoning.typography: Generic sans-serif system with no character — could be any SaaS product. Headline sizing is competent but completely forgettable, lacks typographic voice or discipline.
reasoning.hierarchy: Headline dominates initially but the giant AI-generated hero image competes heavily for attention. Email input feels lost in the composition. Weak visual prioritization overall.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Completely generic fintech template. 'Radically different' claim contradicted by utterly conventional design. Swap Mercury for any neobank and nothing changes — pure stencil.
reasoning.color: Restrained palette of grays/whites with blue accent CTA is appropriate, but the overwhelming decorative hero image breaks any discipline. The image color palette dominates everything.
reasoning.density: Massive amounts of whitespace with anemic content. Single headline, subhead, and email field floating in void. Timid and generic padding ratios — no confidence in density.
reasoning.voice: Standard SaaS homepage template: hero headline + subhead + email capture. IA completely disconnected from 'radically different' promise. Pure conversion funnel stencil with zero structural personality.

winning_patterns: Clean, uncluttered nav with clear hierarchy
Accent color used sparingly on CTAs only
Confident white text on complex background handles legibility adequately
anti_patterns: AI-generated hero image screams 'generic fintech trying too hard' — the desk-in-nature cliché
Claiming 'radically different' while using the most conventional fintech design template possible
Weak headline typography with no character or discipline
Email capture as primary CTA feels low-commitment for 'banking' claim
Enormous hero taking 100vh with almost no actual content
Completely interchangeable with Brex, Ramp, or any neobank homepage
_note: Dark hero, neon accent strokes, big-number callouts
